themes.text: In Italia Per gli indirizzi e recapiti delle Ambasciata e dei Consolati del Paese accreditati in l’Italia, consulta il sito: https://www.esteri.it/it/ministero/rappresentanze-straniere/

fi.levelLabel: Geen herkenbare Turvallisuustaso-formulering gevonden bij um.fi.

themes.heading: Muut turvallisuustason tiedot
themes.text: Ulkoministeriö kehottaa välttämään kaikkea matkustamista Libyaan. Jos kuitenkin olet alueella, noudata äärimmäistä varovaisuutta, seuraa paikallisviranomaisten ohjeita, tee matkustusilmoitus ja ole yhteydessä omaisiisi. UM:n matkustustiedotteet ovat suosituksia Vastaat matkapäätöksestäsi aina itse. Paikallisilla viranomaisilla on ensisijainen vastuu kaikkien maan alueella oleskelevien ihmisten turvallisuudesta ja hätätilanteissa etsintä- ja pelastustoimista. Kriisitilanteissa noudata paikallisten viranomaisten ohjeita. Seuraa uutisointia tiedotusvälineissä sekä tiedotusta edustuston verkkosivuilla ja sosiaalisen median kanavissa. Ulkoministeriö ja Suomen ulkomaanedustustot avustavat suomalaisia konsulipalvelulain mukaisesti. Mikäli Euroopan unionin ulkopuolisessa kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, voit kääntyä muiden EU-maiden edustustojen puoleen. Matkatoimiston järjestämällä matkalla olevaa avustaa ensisijaisesti matkanjärjestäjä. Konsulipalvelulaki Konsulisuojeludirektiivi Laki matkapalveluyhdistelmistä Suosittelemme aina matkavakuutuksen ottamista. Tarkista ennen matkaasi matkavakuutuksesi kattavuus ja voimassaolo erityisesti jos sinulla on jokin sairaus, olet raskaana, matkasi suuntautuu vaarallisille alueille tai harrastat riskialttiita lajeja, Sairastuneen tai vainajan kotiuttaminen on kallista ja vaikea järjestää ilman kattavaa matkavakuutusta, eikä hoitoa aina saa ulkomailla ilman maksusitoumusta tai ennakkomaksua. Tee matkustusilmoitus osoitteessa https://matkustusilmoitus.fi/

themes.heading: Yleinen turvallisuustilanne
themes.text: Yleinen turvallisuustilanne on erittäin heikko ja arvaamaton. Turvallisuustilanne saattaa muuttua nopeasti. Konfliktin osapuolet ovat solmineet lokakuussa 2020 tulitauon. Tästä huolimatta maassa on runsaasti aseistettuja ryhmiä ja aseellisia yhteenottoja esiintyy eri puolilla maata. Tripolissa ainoa toiminnassa oleva kansainvälinen lentokenttä (Mitiga) on myös ollut aseellisten hyökkäysten kohteena. Lentokenttiä voidaan sulkea lyhyellä vartoitusajalla ja lentoja perua. Terrori-iskut ovat myös mahdollisia. Seuraa paikallisviranomaisten ohjeita sekä mediaa. Vältä liikkumista pimeän aikaan sekä väkijoukkoja. Varovaisuus ja valppaus ovat tarpeen koko maassa. Kidnappauksen uhka kohdistuu erityisesti länsimaalaisiin. Vedestä ja elintarvikkeista saattaa ajoittain olla pula. Internet- ja matkapuhelinyhteydet toimivat ajoittain heikosti. Koko maassa esiintyy toistuvia sähkökatkoksia. Suomen kunniakonsulaatti on ollut väliaikaisesti suljettuna 31.12.2015 lähtien. Maassa on tällä hetkellä edustettuna muutama EU-maa, joiden edustustojen/konsulaattien yhteystiedot löytyvät Euroopan komission verkkosivuilta.

themes.heading: Erityiset turvallisuusriskit
themes.text: Kidnappausriski erityisesti ulkomaalaisia kohtaan on olemassa.

themes.heading: Rikollisuus
themes.text: Jos passisi katoaa tai varastetaan, tee siitä ilmoitus paikalliselle poliisiasemalle. Mikäli kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, hae hätämatkustusasiakirjaa (ETD = Emergency Travel Document) toisen EU-maan edustustosta. Ota kopiot tärkeimmistä asiapapereista kuten passista ja lentolipuista. Säilytä ne erillään alkuperäisistä.

themes.heading: Terveys
themes.text: Terveyspalvelujen taso on heikko. Pätevästä hoitohenkilökunnasta on pulaa kaikkialla samoin kuin lääkkeistä ja muista sairaalatarvikkeista. Selvitä ennakkoon matkakohteesi tartuntatautitilanne ja rokotussuositukset. Löydät ne seuraavien viranomaisten verkkosivuilta: Matkailijan terveysopas Maailman terveysjärjestö WHO Euroopan tartuntatautivirasto

themes.heading: Maan lait ja käytännöt
themes.text: Valokuvaamiseen ja videokuvaamiseen liittyy monissa maissa rajoituksia. Mahdollisia kuvauskieltoja on syytä noudattaa. Yleensä esim. sotilaallisten laitosten, lentokenttien tai satamien valokuvaaminen ja videokuvaaminen on kielletty, ja kielto koskee myös ilmakuvausta drooneilla. Myös droonien maahantuonti voi vaatia erillisiä lupaprosesseja. Varmista tarvittaessa rajoitukset etukäteen paikallisviranomaisilta. Kiellettyjä kohteita kuvaava henkilö saatetaan ottaa kiinni tai pidättää, ja kuvauslaitteet saatetaan tuhota tai takavarikoida.

themes.heading: Maahantulosäännökset

Varmista ajantasaiset maahantulosäädökset kohdemaan viranomaisilta, esimerkiksi maan lähimmästä edustustosta. Lue lisää Suomen kansalaisen matkustusasiakirjoista osoitteessa https://um.fi/passi/

themes.text: Reisewarnung (Sicherheitsstufe 4) im ganzen Land! Vor Reisen nach Libyen wird gewarnt. Hinweis: Reisewarnungen werden im Regelfall nur in besonderen Krisensituationen ausgesprochen, wenn eine generelle Gefährdung für Leib und Leben besteht. Reisende, die sich in ein Gebiet mit Reisewarnung begeben, müssen sich der möglichen Konsequenzen bewusst sein: Konsularische Hilfeleistung für in Not geratene Österreicherinnen und Österreicher ist nur äußerst eingeschränkt möglich. Bei Reisen in Gebiete mit Reisewarnung können Versicherungen trotz eines aufrechten Versicherungsvertrages Ausschlussgründe geltend machen und sind damit leistungsfrei. Das Konsulargebührengesetz ermächtigt die Republik Österreich, die Kosten für allenfalls erforderliche Schutzmaßnahmen bzw. Hilfeleistung in bestimmten Situationen im Regressweg von Reisenden zurückzufordern!

For ambulance, fire or police phone: 1415 Depending what you need, contact your: family and friends travel agent insurance provider employer airline Latest update We continue to advise do not travel to Libya due to the dangerous security situation and the high threat of terrorism and kidnapping. If you're in Libya, you should leave as soon as possible if it's safe to do so. Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. Our ability to provide consular assistance to Australians in Libya is severely limited (see 'Safety'). Advice Level summary We advise: Do not travel to Libya due to the dangerous security situation and the high threat of terrorism and kidnapping.

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: Do not travel to Libya. Armed conflict between groups continues to occur in all parts of the country. The security situation is very unstable. There's a high threat of terrorist attack, kidnapping and crime. Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. If you're in Libya, leave as soon as possible if it's safe to do so. Ongoing instability limits our ability to provide consular assistance to Australians. If you choose to stay in Libya, seek professional security advice. Monitor the media for updated information about the security situation. Areas of Libya that border Algeria, Niger, Chad and Sudan are very dangerous. Bandits and armed groups operate in these areas. There's also a very high threat of kidnapping. If you are in a conflict zone, shelter in place until it is safe to leave, carry emergency supplies including food and water, and ensure your travel documents are valid and kept secure. It's illegal to drink alcohol in Libya. If you decide to consume alcohol despite our advice, you should be alert to the potential risks around drink spiking and methanol poisoning from consuming alcoholic drinks. Libya experiences severe weather. If you travel to Libya, follow regional weather forecasts and plan accordingly. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Waterborne, foodborne, parasitic and other infectious diseases are common. They include Leishmaniasis, West Nile virus, typhoid, hepatitis, tuberculosis, plague and measles. It's not safe to drink tap water or to swim in freshwater. Only drink bottled water with sealed lids or boiled water. Medical facilities are limited. Some medicines are in short supply, particularly for chronic conditions. Carry some medical supplies for personal use. If you're seriously ill or injured, you may need to be evacuated. Make sure your insurance covers this. Medical evacuations can be expensive and hard to organise. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: The rule of law is not established in Libya. Police services may not operate in all areas. Penalties for drug offences are severe. They include the death penalty or life imprisonment. This also applies to other serious offences like murder. Libya may not recognise dual citizenship. Women without a male guardian may be restricted in their ability to travel. Officials may seize your passport if you're involved in a business dispute. They may not let you leave Libya until the dispute is settled. Many activities are illegal in Libya. This includes same-sex relationships and drinking alcohol. You could be jailed for having sex outside marriage. Islamic law decides child custody. A child born to a Libyan father is a Libyan citizen. They need their father's permission to leave Libya. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: A visa is required to enter Libya. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate of Libya for the latest details. We can't advise you on the safety of departure options. If you plan to leave, you need to determine the safest route out of the country. In areas where conflict occurs, the best course of action may be to stay where you are until it becomes safer to leave. Airports and land border crossings can close without warning. Plan for delays and temporary closures. Trying to leave Libya via land borders is very dangerous. There's a high threat of kidnapping in regions bordering Algeria, Chad, Sudan and Niger. Don't try to leave Libya through these countries or through illegal border crossings. Officials may detain you if you can't prove you entered Libya at an official border crossing. You must always carry ID documents. Upon arrival, you need to register your passport with a police station. If you don't, you could be fined. Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: There are limits to what consular assistance we can provide in high-risk locations. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. If you choose to travel or stay in Libya despite our advice, our ability to provide consular services in Libya is extremely limited. Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. For consular advice, contact the Australian Embassy in Rome, Italy. Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Armed conflict
themes.text: Armed conflict is occurring across the country, including in Tripoli. Fighting can break out anywhere without warning. If you're in Libya, your life and safety could be at risk. Some foreigners have been killed. Militias have targeted ports, airports, and oil and gas installations. If you travel to Libya despite our advice: leave Libya as soon as it's safe to do so monitor local and international media for possible risks seek professional security advice arrange personal security measures

themes.heading: Consular services
themes.text: Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. Only a few diplomatic missions and international bodies are in Libya. Our ability to provide consular services in Libya is severely limited. For consular advice, contact the Australian Embassy in Rome, Italy.

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: Terrorism is a threat worldwide. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere and at any time. Be alert to possible threats, especially in public places. Terrorism remains a serious threat in Libya, particularly in Fezzan and south of Sirte. Terrorist groups engage in ongoing clashes. Terrorists often use small arms, rocket-propelled grenades or improvised bombs. They target Libyan authorities and security interests. Attacks on foreign interests have also occurred, including: hotels and restaurants locations visited by foreigners, including shopping markets public transportation systems remaining diplomatic interests international non-government organisations To stay safe: check the media for new threats take official warnings seriously If there’s a terrorist incident, leave the area if it’s safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping occurs across the world with political, ideological and criminal motives. Foreigners, including Australians, have been kidnapped overseas whilst travelling. Kidnaps can happen anywhere, even in places that seem safe. Kidnapping occurs in all regions of Libya, and foreign nationals are frequently targeted. A variety of criminal groups use kidnapping for financial or political gain, particularly in urban areas along Libya’s coastline. Militia checkpoints throughout the country’s interior also act as prime locations for potential kidnapping. If despite our advice you travel to areas with a high risk of kidnapping, our ability to provide consular assistance in these destinations is extremely limited. You’re responsible for your own safety. You can find more information on how to reduce your risk of kidnapping here: Kidnapping The Australian Government's longstanding policy is that it doesn't make payments or concessions to kidnappers. Ransom payments to kidnappers have funded further terrorist attacks and criminal activity. Ransom payments to terrorist groups are likely to contravene Australian counter-terrorism financing laws. More information: Kidnapping Scams that affect travellers

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Libya's political situation remains volatile. The rule of law is not established in Libya. Outbreaks of violence can occur with little warning, including in Tripoli. Public protests and events that draw large groups of people can turn violent. Protesters have previously set fire to government buildings. If you travel to Libya despite our advice: avoid protests and demonstrations remain vigilant monitor the media and other sources for possible unrest or strikes More information: Protests and civil unrest

themes.heading: Violent crime
themes.text: Crime levels in Libya are high. Law and order has broken down in most of the country. Violent crime is widespread. Foreigners are at high risk of violent crime. Crimes include carjackings targeting foreign-owned vehicles, extortion and robbery by armed groups.

themes.heading: Border-area violence
themes.text: Areas of Libya that border with Algeria, Niger, Chad and Sudan are very dangerous. Avoid cross-border travel between Libya and these countries. Bandits and armed groups operate in these areas. There's also a very high threat of kidnapping. Violence and armed robberies have been reported as vehicles leave Libya by road. This includes roads that cross into Tunisia.

themes.heading: Extreme weather
themes.text: Libya experiences extreme weather. Libya's rainy season is from November to March. Temperatures can reach 40°C between June and September. If, despite our advice, you decide to travel to or stay in Libya, follow regional weather forecasts and plan accordingly. Register with the Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System to receive alerts on major disasters.

themes.heading: Travel Insurance
themes.text: As Libya is a ‘do not travel’ location, standard travel insurance is unlikely to provide coverage. You should ensure your travel insurance specifically covers high-risk destinations including Libya. You need special travel insurance if you’re going to a high-risk destination, which can be expensive. We can’t guarantee your safety and security in another country or pay for your medical expenses, including evacuation. If you don’t have insurance, you shouldn’t travel.

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: Infectious diseases
themes.text: Waterborne, foodborne, parasitic and other infectious diseases are common. These include: typhoid hepatitis tuberculosis plague measles leishmaniasis West Nile virus Serious outbreaks sometimes occur. To protect yourself from illness: drink boiled water or bottled water with sealed lids don't swim in fresh water take precautions against flea bites don't handle dead animals If you suspect food poisoning, seek urgent medical attention. Also seek medical attention if you have a fever or diarrhoea. More information: Infectious diseases

themes.heading: Medical care
themes.text: You'll need to be evacuated if you get seriously ill or injured. Medical evacuation may not be possible and can be very expensive. Most Australian travel insurance policies won't cover you for travel to Libya. Medical facilities in Libya are limited. Some medications are in short supply. Medical facilities in remote areas can be basic or non-existent. Carry some medical supplies for personal use.

themes.heading: LGBTQIA+ travellers
themes.text: Libyan law prohibits same-sex relationships. Same-sex marriage is also illegal. Convicted offenders face prison sentences. More information: Advice for LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for drug offences are severe. They include the death penalty or life in jail. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: Local customs
themes.text: The Islamic holy month of Ramadan is observed in Libya. Respect religious and cultural customs and laws at this time. During Ramadan, eating, drinking and smoking may be illegal in public during the day. If you're not fasting, avoid these activities around people who are. Seek local advice to avoid offence.

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visa conditions
themes.text: If, despite our advice, you decide to travel to Libya, you need a visa. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate for details about visas, currency, customs and other travel requirements.

themes.heading: Border measures
themes.text: Officials may detain you if you can't prove you entered at an official border crossing. We cannot offer advice on the safety of individual departure options, including any flights that become available, or on travel to a departure point. You should make your own judgement about the safest route out of the country, taking account of the advice of local authorities.

themes.heading: Passport registration
themes.text: If you travel to Libya despite our advice, you must register your passport with a police station on arrival. If it's safe to do so, go to the police station closest to the place you're staying. Failure to do so may result in fines and problems during your stay or on departure.

themes.heading: Yellow fever vaccination
themes.text: You need a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate to enter Libya if you arrive from a country with a risk of yellow fever. Some airlines may want to see one when you leave. More information: Countries with a risk of yellow fever

themes.heading: Travel via Israel
themes.text: If your passport shows entry to Israel, you won't be able to enter Libya.

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Passport with ‘X’ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The local currency is the Libyan Dinar (LYD). Libya law prohibits importing or exporting the Libyan Dinar. Libya is a cash-based economy. Debit cards, credit cards and traveller’s cheques are not often accepted, and ATMs are rare. Official exchange offices will not accept Australian dollars. Withdrawing money from banks may be difficult due to severe shortages of cash. The security situation sometimes disrupts banking services.

themes.heading: Landmines
themes.text: Unexploded remnants of war, including landmines, are a hazard throughout Libya.

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Road travel within Libya is dangerous. There's a risk of carjacking and robbery as well as being caught up in outbreaks of localised violence. Don't attempt to leave Libya via borders with Algeria, Chad, Sudan, or Niger. There's a very high threat of kidnapping. All border crossings can close without warning. Plan for delays and temporary road closures. The road between Tripoli and Zawiya is vulnerable to attack. Convoys, including diplomatic convoys, have been attacked. If you plan to cross the border to Tunisia at the Ras Jedir checkpoint, plan your journey carefully. Check the border crossing is open before you leave. Be prepared for armed clashes at or on the way to the border. Road traffic accidents are common and the emergency response to a road traffic accident may be limited. Road conditions vary across the country. Driving standards are poor and traffic is heavy. In the desert, roads are unpaved and wind-blown sand can reduce visibility quickly. There may be fuel shortages and long queues at fuel stations.

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Air travel and airport operations are subject to disruption. Check with your travel provider about route options and airport operations at your planned point of arrival or departure. Check Libya's air safety profile with the Aviation Safety Network. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Emergencies
themes.text: Depending on what you need, contact your: family and friends travel agent insurance provider employer airline Emergency services exist in Libya, but you shouldn't expect them to help you. For ambulance, fire or police phone: 1415 The rule of law has not been re-established in Libya. Police and medical services may not be operating in all parts of the country.

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: There are limits to what consular assistance we can provide in high-risk locations. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. Ongoing unrest limits our consular help to Australians who are in Libya. We don't have an Australian diplomatic mission in Libya. For consular advice, contact the Australian Embassy in Rome, Italy.

themes.heading: Australian Embassy, Rome
themes.text: Via Antonio Bosio 5 00161 Rome, Italy Phone: +39 06 85 2721 Email: consular-rome@dfat.gov.au Website: Australia in Italy Facebook: Australian Embassy, Italy X: @AusAmbItaly

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: 기본정보 (basisinformatie)
themes.text: ㅇ 국명 : 리비아(State of Libya) ㅇ 수도 : 트리폴리(Tripoli) ㅇ 면적 : 176만km2 (한반도의 8배) ㅇ 언어 : 아랍어 ㅇ 인종 : 아랍인(48%), 베르베르족(20%), 투아레그족(12%), 소수민족(20%) ㅇ 종교 : 이슬람교(수니) ㅇ 화폐 : 리비안디나르(Libyan Dinar, LYD) [제2차내전(동-서 대치 구도 형성과 고착화] ㅇ 2011.10.23. 국가과도위원회(NTC)는 카다피 정권 붕괴 후 리비아 해방을 선언 ㅇ 2012.7.7. 총선 실시에 따라 제헌의회(GNC)가 새로 구성되어 국가과도위원회(NTC)를 대체 ㅇ 2014.2.14. 리비아국민군(LNA)은 제헌의회(GNC)의 해산과 새로운 과도정부 수립을 목표로 거병을 선언, 이후 이슬람 민병대에 대한 군사 행동을 개시 ㅇ 2015.12.17. 유엔 주도로 리비아 정치합의(스키라츠 정치합의) 타결, 이후 합의에 따라 국민통합정부(GNA) 내각 출범 ㅇ 2017.12.17. 리비아국민군은 GNA의 합법성을 인정할 수 없다고 선언하, 리비아 남부 및 동부로 세력 확장 ㅇ 2021.3.10., 유엔의 주도로 진행된 리비아 정치대화(LPDF)의 결과로 새로운 임시통합정부(GNU) 출범 ㅇ 임시통합정부는 2021.12.24. 이전 선거 실시를 임무로 하였으나, 아직까지 대선 및 총선이 미실시 상태이며, 최근 동부와 서부 주요 정치세력 간 2027.2월 내 전국 선거를 실시하기로 합의

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고 현황
themes.text: ㅇ 2020년 기준 리비아내 범죄현황 : 살인 376건, 납치 242건, 살인미수 48건, 강도 1,058건 발생 ※ 리비아 정부기능 및 공권력의 원활한 집행이 이루어지지 않아 사건사고에 대한 통계 비정기적으로 이루어지고 있음 (마지막 국가통계자료를 2020년 발표)

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: ㅇ 내외국인을 막론한 납치, 강도, 살인, 차량강탈 등 총기를 이용한 사건사고가 빈번하게 발생하고 있으며 마약 및 주류 밀매 등의 사건도 증가하는 추세 - 민병세력, 지역 범죄조직 등에 의한 강도, 납치, 차량강탈 등 각종 범죄가 만연

themes.heading: 유의해야할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 현재 리비아 정부기능 및 공권력의 미비로 다양한 사건사고가 빈번히 발생하고 있으며 민간인도 총기를 소지하고 있는 경우가 많아 전 지역에서 안전에 극히 유의해야 함 ㅇ 최근 트리폴리 내 무장세력들이 불법이주자 단속과 추방을 지속하면서 현지 치안이 악화되고 사회적 불안이 심화되는 중 ㅇ 특히 자위야(Al Zawiya) 지역에선 2026년 봄부터 현지 무장세력 간 교전이 지속되어 인근 지역 이동 시 안전에 유의할 필요

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : X ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : X ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : X ㅇ 도착사증 발급 여부 : X 입국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 민병대들의 교전이나 무력 충돌이 발생하는 지역이므로, 개인 신번 안전에 각별 유의 필요 ㅇ 주류, 돼지고기 반입금지 ㅇ 여행금지국가로 입국 시 외교부의 사전 허가 필요 ㅇ외화 반입 현금이 $10,000 이상시 세관에 신고한 후 신고서를 수령 지참하여 입국 출국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 외화를 반출할 때는 입국시 신고한 신고서 또는 은행에서 발급한 환전증명서를 세관에 제출 참고자료 ㅇ 해당 국가의 주한공관홈페이지 ㅇ 해당국가의 한국 대사관 홈페이지 : overseas.mofa.go.kr/ly-ko/index.do ㅇ 기타 참고자료 : X

themes.heading: 일반문화
themes.text: ㅇ 대부분의 리비아 국민들은 이슬람 율법에 따른 종교생활을 실천 - 종교생활을 해치는 행위를 삼가고 리비아 여성과 접촉을 삼가야 함 - 외국인 여성의 경우 노출이 심한 옷 착용은 피하는 것이 좋음 ㅇ 음주, 도박, 고리대금, 돼지고기 등은 금지되어 있으며 초대받지 않은 경우 리비아인 집 방문은 삼가야 함 ㅇ 비무슬림의 모스크 출입은 금지사항 ㅇ 종교에 따른 금식 기간 라마단에는 일출에서 일몰시까지 금식 - 현지인 앞에서 물을 마시거나 음식을 먹는 것이 금지사항은 아니나 되도록 피하는 것이 좋음 - 금식기간 동안에 예민하게 반응할 수 있으므로 시비가 발생하는 일이 없도록 주의하고 특히, 난폭운전이 많으므로 차량사고에 주의

themes.heading: 종교관련
themes.text: ㅇ 리비아는 이슬람을 국교로 하고 있으며 국민 대부분이 이슬람 수니파임 ㅇ 타 종교활동을 금지하고 있지 않으나 집회 장소나 대외적 활동 등은 극히 제한됨 ㅇ 타 종교의 선교활동은 인정하지 않으며, 특히 샤리아 이슬람 종교법에 의해 무슬림에 대한 선교활동은 엄격히 제한되며 선교활동 적발 시 처벌될 수 있음

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: ㅇ 일부 쉬운 영어가 통용되나 간단한 아랍어를 사용하면 상대방과 더 친근한 분위기를 조성할 수 있음 - 다만 리비아인들을 자극할 수 있는 국내 정치 및 종교 문제 등은 삼갈 필요

themes.heading: 교통정보
themes.text: ㅇ 2026.7월 현재 리비아는 여행 금지국으로 지정되어 있으며, 교통정보는 리비아 치안상황 호전으로 항공 등 교통상황이 정상화 될 경우 업데이트 예정임

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 트리폴리를 비롯한 지중해 연안 지역은 비교적 온화한 지중해성 기후로 우기인 겨울철을 제외하고는 건조하면서도 화창한 날씨 지속 - 여름철에는 간혹 불어오는 모래바람 사막의 무더운 바람으로 극심한 모래 먼지를 동반하여 순식간에 주변기온을 10-15 상승시킴 ㅇ 여름철에는 주.야간 일교차가 심하여 감기약을 준비하는 것이 좋으며 낮에는 강한 햇빛 때문에 선글라스 착용 및 자외선 차단 로션을 바르는 것이 좋음 ㅇ 리비아내 특별한 자연재해는 없었으나, 배수시설이 잘되어 있지 않아 겨울철 우기에는 도로에 침수 구간이 많이 나타남

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: ㅇ 현재 리비아는 여행금지국으로 지정되어 있어 리비아에 입국하기 위해서는 우리 정부로부터 예외적 여권 사용에 대한 사전허가를 받은 뒤 입국하여야 함 ㅇ 리비아와 대한민국은 사증면제협정이 체결되어 있지 않아 리비아 방문 시 사전에 반드시 비자를 취득해야 하며 비자를 받기까지 장기간(경우에 따라 1개월 이상)이 소요됨 - 리비아 해외공관은 비자 발급 시 대부분 본국으로부터 승인(일명 비자케이블)을 받은 후 형식적인 발급 절차만 시행
